questions.q: A charge of +2 μC is placed at the origin. The direction of the electric field at a point (x, y) where the magnitude of the electric field is 2 × 10^3 NC^-1?
questions.explanation: The direction of the electric field is towards the positive charge.
questions.topic: Electric Field due to a Point Charge

questions.q: A 2 μC charge is placed at a distance of 4 cm from an uncharged conducting sphere. The magnitude of the charge on the sphere is?
questions.explanation: The charge will be distributed on the surface of the sphere, and it will be equal and opposite to the given charge.
questions.topic: Electric Field due to a Charged Conductor

questions.q: A point charge of 4 μC is placed at the origin. The electric potential at a point (2, 3) is?
questions.explanation: The electric potential at a point is given by V = kq / r, where r is the distance between the charge and the point.
questions.topic: Electric Potential due to a Point Charge

questions.q: A 3 μC charge is placed at a distance of 5 cm from an uncharged metal sphere. The electric potential on the surface of the sphere is?
questions.explanation: The electric potential at a point is given by V = kq / r, where r is the distance between the charge and the point.
questions.topic: Electric Field and Potential due to a Charged Conductor

questions.explanation: The direction of the electric field is towards the positive charge.
